items.title: Coinbase's corporate customers can now accept payments from AI agents
items.description: Rolling out this week, Coinbase Business users will be able to accept payments from AI agents, done via the x402 protocol, developed and incubated by the exchange.

items.title: The SEC settles with Coinbase over its missing Gary Gensler texts
items.description: A new court status report reveals the agency will pay a flat fee to resolve a two-year fight over what former Chair Gary Gensler knew about Ethereum.

items.title: Crypto for Advisors: It’s time for tokenization to get to work
Uniswap pushes deeper into tokenized assets with permissioned trading pools
items.description: The framework, developed with Superstate, Securitize and Dowgo, lets regulated funds and securities trade on Uniswap while enforcing compliance rules necessary for institutions.

items.title: Goldman Sachs CEO backs Clarity Act despite banking industry's concerns over stablecoin rules
items.description: The Goldman Sachs CEO said the crypto market structure bill would create a more stable regulatory framework, breaking with other major bank leaders who oppose key stablecoin provisions.

items.title: The KIDS Act is way worse than digital carding — it is a mass surveillance system
items.description: The fact that lawmakers are even contemplating a bill that would create a surveillance and censorship regime should be a wake-up call for everyone who values privacy and free expression, argues Electronic Frontier Foundation's India McKinney.

items.title: BlackRock, Coinbase, Strategy in a new group pledging $15 million to prepare Bitcoin for quantum threats
items.description: Members will direct funding independently, with the consortium taking no role in Bitcoin governance or protocol decisions.

items.title: Abu Dhabi's Mubadala Capital joins tokenization push as Coinbase takes stake in onchain fund
items.description: The sovereign wealth-backed asset manager tapped KAIO to bring one of its private market funds onchain across Base, Solana and Sui networks.

items.title: Goldman Sachs CEO backs ‘not perfect’ CLARITY Act as vote expected soon
items.description: Goldman chief David Solomon will support the crypto market structure bill now in Congress, even as financial companies and banks are hesitant over provisions on stablecoins.

items.title: Here’s why the CLARITY Act’s ethics deal may be so hard to reach
items.description: Both parties say they want US crypto market structure legislation, but a dispute over ethics rules and who enforces them is becoming the bill’s biggest obstacle.

items.title: BTC treasury firm Empery Digital invests $20M in AI data center developer Cardinal Data Power
items.description: The investment marks Empery’s latest push into AI infrastructure as the company shifts capital away from its Bitcoin treasury strategy.

items.title: Ethereum nears market bottom against Bitcoin, though key signals remain unconfirmed: CryptoQuant
items.description: Ether trades below its realized price while onchain indicators point to easing selling pressure and recovering demand, though a definitive cycle bottom has yet to emerge.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.description: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: Fears of AI-driven DeFi hack epidemic overstated for now — but not for long
items.description: Are the fears of an AI driven hacking epidemic totally overblown, or is this just the lull before the storm?

items.title: Binance joins STOP THE TRAFFIK in anti-human trafficking effort
items.description: The partnership expands Binance’s compliance efforts by providing intelligence and training to help detect cryptocurrency activity tied to human trafficking and child exploitation.

items.title: Strategy-led group pledges $15M to quantum-proof Bitcoin network
items.description: A consortium led by Strategy and BlackRock has pledged $15 million to secure the Bitcoin network from the threat of a quantum computing breakthrough.

items.title: Shortsighted stock market can no longer brush off war: 'It's too hard to ignore $100 oil'
items.description: After staying flat as the U.S.-Iran war heated up again, equities tumbled on Thursday after oil prices crossed $100 per barrel.

items.title: Congress splits on war powers resolutions to force Trump to abandon Iran war
items.description: Trump would be nearly certain to veto any measure to curtail his ability to wage war with Iran.

items.title: Amazon and Microsoft pivot cloud gaming strategies to target different players
items.description: Amazon is bringing Luna to the Prime Video streaming service, while Microsoft will test an ad-supported streaming of titles that people own.

items.title: As Honda CR-V leads U.S. sales, automaker teases new American-built pickup truck
items.description: The future of the Ridgeline midsize pickup truck had been in flux, and the automaker said there will be a temporary production stoppage for the truck.

items.title: Tesla falls 13%, Alphabet sinks 7% as AI spending concerns spook investors
items.description: Both Tesla and Alphabet signaled higher spending as they invest in artificial intelligence.

items.title: House lawmakers grill former JPMorgan executive Jes Staley over Epstein ties
items.description: House oversight Chair James Comer said Jes Staley defended Jeffrey Epstein and encouraged JPMorgan to retain him despite internal red flags.

items.title: Google Cloud CEO Kurian says customers are spending 50% more as segment blows away expectations
items.description: Google Cloud CEO Thomas Kurian told CNBC on Thursday that the company's existing customers are spending "roughly 50% more" than they've already committed.

items.title: A tax break for preserving land has drawn IRS scrutiny. Here’s when it still makes sense
items.description: For wealthy landowners, conservation easements can still make sense if you avoid these IRS red flags.

items.title: Bitcoin muted at $65k with Clarity Act progress in focus
Crypto exchange BitMEX to shut down operations in September
Bitcoin coils near $65,700 in tight range: Hourly levels
Adam Weitsman Backs Unserious in their Acquisition of Creepz and Psychrome homecoming
Bitcoin muted around $66k amid concerns over key regulatory bill, Iran tensions

Bybit Presents Exclusive $1M Prize Pool for Stock-Themed Trading As Wall Street Enters Earnings Season

items.transcript_reason:
Could not retrieve a transcript for the video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=30bZLVVVHaI! This is most likely caused by:

YouTube is blocking requests from your IP. This usually is due to one of the following reasons:
- You have done too many requests and your IP has been blocked by YouTube
- You are doing requests from an IP belonging to a cloud provider (like AWS, Google Cloud Platform, Azure, etc.). Unfortunately, most IPs from cloud providers are blocked by YouTube.

There are two things you can do to work around this:
1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: Most FDA advisors say the BPC-157 peptide should be made available in the U.S. Here’s what happens next.
items.description: The FDA is deciding whether seven peptides like BPC-157 and TB-500 can be legally compounded, potentially opening the door to broader use in Americans.

items.title: Global oil tops $100 after Houthis strike Saudi tankers and Trump threatens ‘military punishment’ on Iran
items.description: Oil futures climbed sharply Thursday, with global benchmark Brent crude topping $100 a barrel for the first time since May, after Yemen’s Houthi militants claimed attacks on two Saudi Arabian tankers in the Red Sea.

items.title: Are you looking for financial advice? This is the best way to ask for it.

items.description: Lockheed Martin’s stock jumps as a ramp up in missile production helped lead to an earnings beat and raise.

items.title: ‘I have a $2,000 deductible’: I reported a cracked windshield on my Hertz rental car. Can they charge me?
items.description: “I never heard a rock hit the windshield.”

items.description: More than 200 years of data show stocks typically gain in August, while market volatility sits well below average.

2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

items.title: US weapons makers’ sales soar as Iran war boosts order backlog
items.description: Pentagon is pushing major defence contractors to increase production rates to rebuild depleted stockpiles

items.title: Greek tycoon’s tanker towed into Iranian waters
items.description: Shipping group Dynacom ‘trying to verify’ whereabouts of vessel hit in Strait of Hormuz

items.title: Oil price surge drives global bond sell-off
items.description: Brent crude’s rise above $100 threatens a prolonged surge in inflation and resetting interest rate expectations

items.title: ECB holds interest rates at 2.25% after debating rise
items.description: Some members of governing council ‘asked themselves whether we should not consider a hike’

items.title: The US may find it hard to shrug off Moonshot’s AI shockwaves
items.description: The latest Chinese models such as Kimi K3 look set to bring greater price competition

items.title: Google burns through $6bn in cash as AI spending climbs again
items.description: Search giant says it will commit up to $205bn to AI investments in 2026

items.title: Trump says Saudi nuclear deal depends on relations with Israel
items.description: Crown prince has insisted any rapprochement with Israel will depend on steps towards establishing Palestinian state

items.title: Europe could help get Ukraine and Russia to the negotiating table
items.description: Only diplomacy will end this war — and Kyiv’s backers should seek to kick-start talks while the US is mired in Iran

items.title: Trump Prepares Yet Another Strategy to Resurrect Global Tariffs
items.description: The administration’s 10 percent global tariffs are set to expire at midnight, but officials have been preparing replacements.

items.title: China Rewrites the ‘Soft Power’ Playbook for the A.I. Age
items.description: China is seeking global influence and good will with its open, low-cost artificial intelligence software.

items.title: ‘Odyssey’ Popcorn Buckets Help Fuel a $100 Million Market
items.description: Once, a tub of popcorn was just a tub of popcorn. Now, ‘collectible concession vessels’ could make one chain more than $100 million.

items.title: Even as War Rages, Investors Bet Oil Will Be Cheaper in a Few Months
items.description: Futures contracts reflect an expectation that low inventories and midterm elections are likely to push the Trump administration to try to ease tensions with Iran.

items.title: Global Oil Price Hits $100 a Barrel for First Time Since May
items.description: The recent rebound reflects traders’ growing concern about the expanding war, which now extends from the Persian Gulf to the Red Sea.

items.title: Ford Will Use Apple Maps in New Self-Driving System
items.description: In a first, Apple software will influence the way a new line of electric vehicles made by Ford Motor operates, and other automakers may follow.

items.title: Edward O’Keefe Named C.E.O. of WNET
items.description: Mr. O’Keefe, who was previously an executive at CNN, is the chief executive of the Theodore Roosevelt Presidential Library.

items.title: Google Sets the Pace on Hefty A.I. Spending
items.description: The technology giant again raised its projections for investing in artificial intelligence. Many on Wall Street still worry about the eventual payoff.
